_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate rogue/unauthorized systems on a network
reference_document_element_description: Ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues that span multiple system layers, tracing problems across application code, data pipelines, databases, networks, and infrastructure to identify and remediate root causes.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.421), DA-T-009 (DA, cosine: 0.418).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.3/10 (raw 0.533). Shared vocabulary: [ability]. DCWF-only terms [evaluate, network, rogue, systems, unauthorized] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, application, causes, code, data, databases] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.858 (P=0.869, R=0.847). P exceeds R by 0.022 — the DCWF element's 8 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 29 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.5%). "Ability to evaluate rogue/unauthorized systems on a network" and "Ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues that span multiple system layer" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.458 < STS 0.533 (gap -0.075). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [evaluate, network, rogue] and [across, application, causes] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.1%. Shared tokens: [ability]. DCWF-only: [evaluate, network, rogue, systems, unauthorized]. WIDAI-only: [across, application, causes, code, data, databases].
